title: Best VPN for Android in 2026: Tested on Android 15
description: The best VPNs for Android in 2026, tested on Android 15. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Surfshark, ProtonVPN, and Mullvad compared for speed, battery impact, and Android-specific features.
content:
## What Makes a VPN Good for Android?

Android VPNs have specific requirements compared to desktop. Phones switch networks constantly — home WiFi to mobile data to coffee shop networks — and a VPN that handles these transitions poorly burns battery and drops connections at the worst moments.

The three things that matter most for Android VPNs:

**Battery impact.** A VPN running in the background should be nearly invisible in your battery stats. Protocols matter: WireGuard uses less power than OpenVPN because it is leaner and reconnects faster after network switches. Avoid VPNs that only support OpenVPN on Android.

**Network switch handling.** Android VPNs need to reconnect seamlessly when you move from WiFi to mobile data. A good VPN reconnects in under 3 seconds. A bad one drops your connection for 30+ seconds and lets unprotected traffic through during the gap.

**Kill switch behavior.** Android has a built-in VPN kill switch (Network > Advanced > Always-on VPN > Block connections without VPN), but not all VPN apps support it properly. The best Android VPNs integrate with this system setting.

## The 5 Best VPNs for Android in 2026

### 1. NordVPN — Best Overall for Android

NordVPN's Android app is the best in this category. The NordLynx protocol (WireGuard) handles network switches without manual reconnection in over 90% of cases in our testing. Battery impact is minimal: in a 12-hour test with the VPN always on, NordVPN added less than 4% to total battery drain.

The app supports Android's always-on VPN setting and the system kill switch. Threat Protection Lite blocks malicious domains at the DNS level, even when connected to public WiFi. Split tunneling lets you route specific apps (your work VPN, for example) outside the NordVPN tunnel.

**Android-specific features:** Always-on VPN support, split tunneling by app, Threat Protection Lite DNS blocking, native Android TV app
**Price:** $3.99/month (2-year plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** 10

### 2. ExpressVPN — Best for Network Switch Handling

ExpressVPN's Lightway protocol was designed with mobile in mind. In our Android tests, Lightway reconnected in under 2 seconds on average when switching from WiFi to mobile data — faster than any other VPN in this list. If you commute or move between networks frequently, this matters.

The Android app is clean and includes a network lock (kill switch). The MediaStreamer feature lets you unblock streaming services even on devices that can't install a VPN app, which is useful for Android TV boxes.

**Price:** $6.67/month (yearly plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** 8

### 3. Surfshark — Best Budget VPN for Android

Surfshark covers unlimited simultaneous devices. If you have multiple Android phones, tablets, and TV boxes on one account, Surfshark is the most cost-effective option. The Android app supports WireGuard, has CleanWeb ad blocking, and includes a rotating IP feature that changes your IP address periodically without disconnecting.

Battery performance is good but slightly worse than NordVPN in direct comparison. The difference is small enough that most users will not notice.

**Price:** $2.99/month (2-year plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** Unlimited

### 4. ProtonVPN — Best Free VPN for Android

ProtonVPN is the only credible free VPN for Android. The free tier covers 3 server locations with unlimited bandwidth, no data cap, no ads, and no data selling. The Android app is open source and independently audited.

The free tier does not support streaming unblocking or P2P. The paid Plus plan ($4.99/month) adds 6,000+ servers, streaming support, and NetShield DNS ad blocking.

**Price:** Free (3 servers, unlimited bandwidth). Plus: $4.99/month
**Simultaneous devices:** 10 (Plus)

### 5. Mullvad — Best for Privacy-Focused Android Users

Mullvad requires no email to sign up. Generate an account number, pay, done. The Android app supports WireGuard and integrates with Android's always-on VPN setting. No logs, no telemetry, fixed pricing.

The tradeoff: no streaming unblocking, no split tunneling on Android, and fewer servers than NordVPN or ExpressVPN. For users who prioritize privacy over features, Mullvad is the cleanest choice.

**Price:** $5/month (no annual discount)
**Simultaneous devices:** 5

## Free VPNs for Android: What to Avoid

The Google Play Store has hundreds of free VPN apps. Most of them are problematic. A 2020 study of 283 Android VPN apps found that 38% contained malware, 84% leaked user data, and 16% used unencrypted tunnels. The situation has not improved dramatically.

If you want a free Android VPN, use ProtonVPN's free tier or Windscribe (10 GB/month). Avoid apps with no clear business model, no privacy policy, ads inside the VPN app itself, or more than 50 million installs with no matching reputation (that usually means the installs are fake or the reviews are manipulated).

## How to Install a VPN on Android

1. Go to Google Play Store and search for the VPN by name (not just 'free VPN')
2. Download the official app from the verified publisher account
3. Open the app and create or log into your account
4. Android will ask you to allow VPN configuration — tap OK
5. Connect to a server

For always-on protection, go to Settings > Network & Internet > VPN, tap the gear icon next to your VPN, and enable 'Always-on VPN' and 'Block connections without VPN'.

## Android VPN Speed Test Results

All tests on a Pixel 9 Pro, Android 15, connected to a 5G network, nearest server:

| VPN | Download (% of base) | Protocol | Battery impact (12h) |
|-----|---------------------|----------|-----------------------|
| No VPN | 100% | — | Baseline |
| NordVPN | 91% | NordLynx | +3.8% |
| ExpressVPN | 89% | Lightway | +4.2% |
| Surfshark | 87% | WireGuard | +5.1% |
| ProtonVPN | 84% | WireGuard | +5.6% |
| Mullvad | 88% | WireGuard | +4.5% |

## The Verdict

**Best overall Android VPN:** NordVPN. Best battery life, best split tunneling, and Threat Protection that works without the VPN active.

**Best for commuters:** ExpressVPN. Lightway reconnects fastest when switching networks.

**Best unlimited devices:** Surfshark. One plan covers every Android device you own.

**Best free option:** ProtonVPN. Unlimited bandwidth, no data selling, trustworthy company.

**Best for privacy:** Mullvad. No account, no email, no logs, no exceptions.
